title: [CI:DOCS] UPDATE manpages with MANPAGE_SYNTAX
body:
The following manpages have been adapted to the MANPAGE_SYNTAX:
- podman-completion
- podman-container-checkpoint
- podman-container-cleanup
- podman-container-exists

The following manpages have had little changes:
- podman-attach
- podman-commit
- MANPAGE_SYNTAX
- Makefile

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Procyhon*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Hey, I am not sure what would be the best practice but you have capitalized the word "option" everywhere... is that really necessary?

> Hey, I am not sure what would be the best practice but you have capitalized the word "option" everywhere... is that really necessary?

The idea behind this was that once a formatting is established, it is also retained in the entire document, so that one can immediately recognize what exactly is being written. For example, if the word option is meant in the sense of a flag, this should be clearly recognizable. However, if the word option is used in a different context, it is written in lower case, as it has nothing to do with the option in the sense of a flag. The same is done with the options themselves, for example. There, too, the `**bold**` notation is retained. It is simply a matter of ensuring uniform formatting for the entire document and also making it clear what the written text is about. I agree that it looks strange at first, but I think the reason behind it should vouch for it.

One tiny nit, @Procyhon if you can fix and repush, this is GTG.
Apologies for the tardy review.
